zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabelle De Blamont於1260年出生在法國德 艾斯特姆特-吉莫瑟勒布拉蒙特時，她的父親Heinrich von Salm-Blamont I23歲，母親Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningen18歲。她於1296年與Eudes IV de Grancey結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1329年在法國布格o-達格朗希勒-a奧享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabelle De Blamont于1260年出生在法国德 艾斯特姆特-吉莫瑟勒布拉蒙特时，她的父亲Heinrich von Salm-Blamont I23岁，母亲Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningen18岁。她于1296年与Eudes IV de Grancey结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1329年在法国布格o-達格朗希勒-a奧享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: Blamont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: from the medieval personal name Lagman, which is from Old Norse Lǫgmathr, composed of the elements lǫg, plural of lag ‘law’ (from leggja ‘to lay down’), + mathr ‘man’ (genitive manns).
French: habitational name, with fused definite article l', from any of the places called Amont, in Haute-Saône and Haute-Vienne.
Altered form of French   Lamothe  .

variants.name: McClammy
meanings: Scottish: patronymic from a pet form of   Lamont  .

variants.name: McClymont
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Laomuinn ‘son of Laomann’, a personal name borrowed from Old Norse and meaning ‘law giver’. See Lamont .

variants.name: Lemond
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lamont  .
French: variant of Lemonde, a nickname from Old French monde ‘pure’, with fused masculine definite article le.
French (Lémond): variant of Laymond, itself a variant, with fused definite article l', of Aymond .

variants.name: Lemont
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lamont  .
French: habitational name from Le Mont in the Vosges or possibly other minor places so named from Old French mont ‘mountain’. Alternatively, a variant of Lemond 2.

variants.name: Lemonds
meanings: Scottish: variant of Lamont , with English excrescent -s.

variants.name: Lawman
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Lamont  .

English: occupational name from Middle English lagman, lagheman ‘law-man, one whose duty is to declare the law; lawyer’ (Old Norse lagmann-, lǫgmann-, oblique case of lǫgmathr). The word was also widely used as a personal name, Middle English Lagman, Lagheman.

variants.name: Lamond
meanings: Scottish, northern Irish, and French: variant of   Lamont  . This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Lamothe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Jacques, Pierre, Donat, Francois, Lucien, Odette, Adrien, Andree, Armand, Armande.
French: variant of   Lamotte  , or a habitational name from any of the places in France named Lamothe or La Mothe.

History: The settlement that became the city of Detroit was founded in 1701 by Antoine de la Mothe, Sieur de Cadillac (1658–1730), governor of LA. He was born into the minor nobility in Gascony, France, where his father owned the seigneury of Cadillac.

variants.name: Lamonte
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lamont  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isabel
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabelle De Blamont was born in 1260, in Blâmont, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Grand Est, France, her father, Heinrich von Salm-Blamont I, was 23 and her mother, Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningen, was 18. She married Eudes IV de Grancey in 1296. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died about 1329, in Grancey-le-Château, Côte-d'Or, Burgundy, France, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabelle De Blamont est née en 1260, à Blâmont, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Grand Est, France, son père, Heinrich von Salm-Blamont I, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningen, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé Eudes IV de Grancey en 1296. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1329, à Grancey-le-Château, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, France, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabelle De Blamont 1260 in Blâmont, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Großer Osten, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Heinrich von Salm-Blamont I 23 und ihre Mutter Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningen 18. Sie hat 1296 Eudes IV de Grancey geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1329 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Grancey-le-Château, Côte-d'Or, Burgund, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabelle De Blamont è nata il nel 1260 nel Blâmont, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Grand Est, Francia, sua padre, Heinrich von Salm-Blamont I, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningen, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato Eudes IV de Grancey nel 1296. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1329, nel Grancey-le-Château, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, Francia, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Blamontが1260年にフランスグランド エストメウルスエ-エト-モセルブルaモントで生まれた時，父Heinrich von Salm-Blamont Iは23歳，母Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningenは18歳であった。1296年にEudes IV de Granceyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1329年にフランスボウルゴグンクoテ-ド'オルグランセイ-レ-チaテアウで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabelle De Blamont(이)가 1260년 프랑스 그랜드 에스트 우르테-트-모셀 블a몽에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Heinrich von Salm-Blamont I(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningen(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1296년 Eudes IV de Grancey(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1329년 프랑스 부르고그니 co테-드'어 그랜스이-레-cha테오에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabelle De Blamont nasceu em 1260, em Blâmont, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Grand Est, França, seu pai, Heinrich von Salm-Blamont I, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningen, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com Eudes IV de Grancey em 1296. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1329, em Grancey-le-Château, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, França, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabelle De Blamont родилась в 1260 г. в местности блaмонт, меурте-ет-моселл, гранд эст, Франция, её отцу по имени Heinrich von Salm-Blamont I было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningen, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Eudes IV de Grancey в 1296 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1329 г. в местности гранси-ле-чaто, кoте-д'ор, бургогн, Франция в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabelle De Blamont nació en 1260, en Blâmont, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Grand Est, Francia, su papá, Heinrich von Salm-Blamont I, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Grafin Kunigunde von Leiningen, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Eudes IV de Grancey en 1296. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1329, en Grancey-le-Château, Côte-d'Or, Bourgogne, Francia, a la edad de 70 años.
